definitions: A thermodynamic quantity that measures the useful work obtainable from an isothermal, isobaric, or isochoric thermodynamic system.
The portion of the internal energy of a system that is available to do work.
examples: The change in free energy determines the spontaneity of a reaction.
Scientists are studying ways to harness free energy from various sources.
Calculating the free energy of the system is crucial for understanding its behavior.
